name: Dissolver
description: Dissolves transients and smears harmonic content into evolving pads. Feed it anything — drums, vocals, guitars — and it extracts the sustained harmonic essence, freezing and blending it into a living, breathing texture.

smoothing.label: Smoothing
smoothing.help: Controls how aggressively transients are suppressed. At 0%, the full attack passes through. At 100%, all percussive onsets are erased, leaving only the sustained harmonic body of the sound.

freeze.label: Freeze
freeze.help: Blends between live spectral content and a frozen snapshot. At 0%, the output follows the input in real-time. At 100%, the harmonic content is held indefinitely, creating a static drone from whatever was last captured.

grain_size.label: Grain Size
grain_size.help: Sets the window size for the granular smearing engine. Small grains (~5ms) preserve more detail and pitch clarity. Large grains (~85ms) create a deep, blurred wash where individual notes merge into texture.

density.label: Density
density.help: Controls how many grains overlap simultaneously. Low density creates a more sparse, flickering texture. High density layers many grains into a thick, organ-like pad.

evolution.label: Evolution
evolution.help: Sets how fast the frozen spectrum drifts and morphs over time. At 0%, the frozen content is static. Higher values introduce slow spectral wandering that keeps the pad alive and breathing.

brightness.label: Brightness
brightness.help: Tilts the spectral balance of the output. At noon (50%), the timbre is neutral. Turn left for darker, warmer pads. Turn right for brighter, airier textures.

mix.label: Dry/Wet
mix.help: Blends between the original input signal and the dissolved pad output. At 100% wet, you hear only the pad. Backing off adds some of the original transients back for rhythmic context.

decay.label: Decay
decay.help: Controls how long the pad tail sustains after the input stops. Short decay lets the pad fade quickly. Long decay creates lingering, reverb-like sustain that can ring for several seconds.

tips: Feed a drum loop and set Smoothing to 100% — the rhythm disappears and you're left with the resonant body of the drums as a pad.
Use Freeze at ~80% with a slow Evolution to capture a vocal phrase and turn it into a slowly shifting drone.
Try small Grain Size with high Density for a shimmering, granular cloud effect.
Large Grain Size with low Density creates a lo-fi, tape-loop-like smearing effect.
Set Mix to ~60% to keep some transient ghost underneath the pad for rhythmic anchoring.
